Rationale: Complete state ownership of means of production and land post-collectivization; private property effectively abolished.
Gosplan central planning dictated all allocation, prices, production; no market mechanisms dominant in 1937.
Enterprises driven by planned targets and quotas, not profit; profit motive suppressed under Stalinist command economy.
Extreme state-controlled redistribution; resources allocated bureaucratically with favoritism to party elite.
Universal nominal access to healthcare/education/employment; severe shortages and privilege-based access reduced effectiveness.
Ideological heredity enforced via party and Komsomol; limited practical variation tolerated if non-threatening.
Minimal economic/political experimentation; Five-Year Plans enforced uniformity.
High institutional persistence through terror and party monopoly in 1937 peak.
Nomenklatura privileges and informal corruption widespread; bureaucratic rent-seeking high despite purges.
State monopolies and quotas eliminated true competition.
System endured intense stresses (famine, purges); short-term viability but long-term strains evident.
Industrialization created superpower niche; at massive human/environmental cost.
Iron Curtain precursors and strict internal controls; limited external openness.
Central planning rigid; feedback loops suppressed by terror.
Denunciation and NKVD quotas weaponized; false positives common, genuine economic cheating persisted.
Highly centralized monolithic structure; little decentralized adaptation.
Party archives centralized; heavily distorted by ideology and fear.
Socialist realism and state media enforced high uniformity.
Marxism-Leninism-Stalinism absolute; dissent equated to treason.
Great Purge at peak; mass arrests/executions via NKVD.
State promoted collective child-rearing; family intervened for loyalty.
Atheism state policy; churches closed, clergy persecuted.
Art required to serve party line; non-conformists repressed.
Science subordinated to state priorities (e.g., Lysenkoism dominant).
Marxist-Leninist indoctrination from kindergarten upward.
Complete state monopoly; Pravda/TASS as propaganda organs.
Massive military buildup prioritized over consumer needs.
High isolation via borders and suspicion of foreigners.
Capitalist encirclement narrative justified purges and controls.
No democratic process; elite selection via purges and terror.
Stalin cult at maximum; infallibility propagated.
Great Purge in full swing; violent elite turnover routine.
Inconsistent; some pro-natal pushes but war losses dominated.
Strict internal passports and border controls.
Russification policies and deportations of suspect groups.
